policies.policy_title: Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial Company - Scale Based Regulation) Directions, 2023
policies.policy_description: Regulatory structure for Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs) based on their size and activity.
policies.scope: All NBFCs registered with the Reserve Bank of India
policies.objective: To regulate NBFCs based on their scale and activity to ensure stability and safety in the financial system

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs are categorized into four layers: Base Layer, Middle Layer, Upper Layer, and Top Layer based on their size and activity.
The Base Layer comprises non-deposit taking NBFCs with asset size below ₹1,000 crore and NBFCs undertaking specific activities.
The Middle Layer consists of deposit-taking NBFCs, non-deposit taking NBFCs with asset size above ₹1,000 crore, and NBFCs undertaking specific activities.
The Upper Layer comprises NBFCs that are specifically identified by the Reserve Bank as warranting enhanced regulatory requirements based on a set of parameters and scoring methodology.
The Top Layer is ideally expected to be empty, but can get populated if the Reserve Bank identifies NBFCs that pose substantial systemic risk.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Categorisation of NBFCs carrying out specific activity
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs undertaking specific activities are categorized into different layers based on their scale and activity.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Multiple NBFCs in a Group - Classification in Middle Layer
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs that are part of a common group or floated by a common set of promoters are consolidated to determine their classification in the Middle Layer.
Once an NBFC reaches an asset size of ₹1,000 crore or above, it is subject to regulatory requirements as per Section III of these Directions.

policies.scope: Applicability of definitions in these directions
policies.objective: To provide clarity on the meanings of various terms used in these directions

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The equity capital and reserves as reduced by intangible assets and revaluation reserves, divided by the number of equity shares of the investee company.
Book value of the assets and interest accrued thereon but not received.
A company registered under section 3 of the Companies Act, 1956 or the corresponding provision under the Companies Act, 2013.

An arrangement involving two or more entities related to each other through any of the following relationships: Subsidiary - parent (defined in terms of AS 21), Joint venture (defined in terms of AS 27), Associate (defined in terms of AS 23), Promoter-promotee [as provided in the SEBI (Acquisition of Shares and Takeover) Regulations, 1997] for listed companies, a related party (defined in terms of AS 18), common brand name, and investment in equity shares of 20 percent and above.

Shall have the same meaning as assigned to it under clause (e) of sub-regulation (1) of regulation 2 of Securities and Exchange Board of India (Substantial Acquisition of Shares and Takeovers) Regulations, 2011.
An investment which is by its nature readily realisable and is intended to be held for not more than one year from the date on which such investment is made.
Interaction between the NBFC and its customers while carrying on its business.

The ratio between the amount of the dividend payable in a year and the net profit as per the audited financial statements for the financial year for which the dividend is proposed. Proposed dividend shall include both dividend on equity shares and compulsory convertible preference shares eligible for inclusion in Tier 1 capital.

The value of an equity share computed by taking the average of profits after tax as reduced by the preference dividend and adjusted for extra-ordinary and non-recurring items, for the immediately preceding three years and further divided by the number of equity shares of the investee company.
The mean of the earning value and the break-up value.

Two or more (natural or legal) persons who satisfy at least one of the following conditions: Control relationship: one person directly or indirectly, has control over the other(s), or such persons are under the common control of a third party (irrespective of whether the NBFC has exposure to the third party or not).
Capital instrument which possesses certain characteristics of equity as well as of debt.

A non-deposit taking NBFC which is permitted to (i) refinance post commencement operations date (COD) infrastructure projects that have completed at least one year of satisfactory commercial operations; and (ii) finance toll operate transfer (TOT) projects as the direct lender.

A credit facility extended by an NBFC to a borrower, by way of term loan, project loan subscription to bonds/debentures/preference shares/equity shares in a project company acquired as a part of the project finance package such that subscription amount to be 'in the nature of advance' or any other form of long term funded facility for exposure in the infrastructure sub-sectors as notified by the Department of Economic Affairs, Ministry of Finance, Government of India, from time to time.

The sum of all exposure values of a NBFC-UL measured in terms of paragraph 110.6 of these Directions, to a counterparty and/or a group of connected counterparties, if it is equal to or above 10 percent of the NBFC-UL's eligible capital base.
An investment other than a current investment.
A person holding 10% or more of the paid-up share capital or five crore rupees in paid-up shares, whichever is lower.

An NBFC as defined in clause (f) of section 45-I of the RBI Act, 1934, which has its principal business as mentioned in paragraph 123 of these Directions and has been granted a certificate of registration under section 3 of the Factoring Regulation Act, 2011.

Any company which is a financial institution carrying on as its principal business - asset finance, the providing of finance whether by making loans or advances or otherwise for any activity other than its own and the acquisition of securities; and is not any other category of NBFCs as defined by the Reserve Bank in any of its Master Directions.
A non-deposit taking NBFC which has a minimum of 75 percent of its total assets deployed towards infrastructure lending.
A non-deposit taking NBFC which has a minimum of 75 percent of its total assets deployed towards 'microfinance loans' as defined under Reserve Bank of India Master Directions.

policies.policy_description: Chapter II Definitions of various terms and concepts related to microfinance loans.
policies.scope: Applicable to all non-deposit taking NBFCs (except NBFCs-BL)
policies.objective: To provide clarity on the definitions of key terms used in the regulatory framework for microfinance loans.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The latest declared net asset value by the mutual fund concerned in respect of that particular scheme.
The aggregate of overdue and future instalments receivable as reduced by the balance of unmatured finance charges and further reduced by the provisions made as per paragraph 15.2 of these directions.
A non-deposit taking NBFC referred to in the 'Guidelines for Licensing of New Banks in the Private Sector' dated February 22, 2013, issued by the Reserve Bank.

The aggregate of paid up equity capital, preference shares which are compulsorily convertible into equity, free reserves, balance in share premium account and capital reserves representing surplus arising out of sale proceeds of asset.

Funds raised either directly or indirectly through public deposits, inter-corporate deposits, bank finance and all funds received from outside sources such as funds raised by issue of Commercial Papers, debentures etc.

Funds raised either directly or indirectly through public deposits, inter-corporate deposits, bank finance and all funds received from outside sources such as funds raised by issue of Commercial Papers, debentures etc.
The Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934 (Act 2 of 1934).
An individual or his spouse or minor child holding a beneficial interest in the shares of a company.
The Reserve Bank of India constituted under section 3 of the RBI Act, 1934.
An individual holding a position equivalent to 'Senior Management' as defined in section 178 of the Companies Act, 2013.
An instrument which is fully paid up, unsecured and subordinated to the claims of other creditors.

The sum of owned fund as reduced by investment in shares of other NBFCs and in shares, debentures, bonds, outstanding loans and advances including hire purchase and lease finance made to and deposits with subsidiaries and companies in the same group.

The sum of preference shares other than those which are compulsorily convertible into equity, revaluation reserves at discounted rate of 55 percent, general provisions and loss reserves, hybrid debt capital instruments, subordinated debt and perpetual debt instruments.

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.scope: This policy applies to all organizational units, personnel, systems, and processes involved in . The scope encompasses all relevant organizational activities, systems, and personnel as determined by the policy requirements. It includes all operational environments and applies to all stakeholders who may be affected by or responsible for policy implementation.
policies.objective: The primary objective of this policy is to establish clear requirements and guidelines for , ensuring organizational compliance and operational effectiveness. Specific objectives include: (1) establishing clear expectations and requirements for organizational behavior; (2) ensuring consistent implementation of best practices across all applicable areas; (3) minimizing operational risks and compliance gaps; (4) promoting accountability and responsibility among all stakeholders; (5) enabling effective monitoring and continuous improvement; and (6) supporting overall organizational objectives and strategic goals.

section_info.title: Chapter III Registration

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: The Reserve Bank specifies a minimum Net Owned Fund (NOF) required for NBFCs to commence or carry on business.
policies.objective: To ensure the financial stability and solvency of NBFCs

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Existing NBFCs must achieve a minimum NOF of ₹10 crore by specified dates.
subpolicies.control: NBFC-ICC, NBFC-MFI, and NBFC-Factor must meet the NOF requirement by March 31, 2025; otherwise, they will not be eligible for Certificate of Registration.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Investments made by NBFCs in entities of the same group or other NBFCs are deducted from NOF calculation.
subpolicies.control: Investments made by NBFCs in entities of the same group or other NBFCs are treated alike, provided the funds come from the NBFC to the extent of 50% or more.

policies.policy_description: New investors from non-compliant jurisdictions cannot acquire significant influence in investee companies.
policies.objective: To prevent money laundering and terrorist financing

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: New investors from non-compliant jurisdictions cannot acquire significant influence in investee companies.
subpolicies.control: Fresh investors from non-compliant jurisdictions must not hold more than 20% of the voting power of the NBFC.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Investors holding investments prior to classification as non-compliant can continue or bring in additional investments.
subpolicies.control: Existing investors from non-compliant jurisdictions may continue their investments or bring in additional investments to support business continuity.

section_info.title: Chapter IV Prudential Regulation

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: The leverage ratio of NBFCs (except NBFC-MFIs, NBFCs-ML and above) shall not be more than seven at any point of time.
policies.objective: To ensure the financial stability of NBFCs

policies.policy_description: NBFCs primarily engaged in lending against gold jewellery shall maintain a minimum Tier 1 capital of 12 percent of aggregate risk weighted assets of on-balance sheet and of risk adjusted value of off-balance sheet items.
policies.objective: To ensure the financial stability of NBFCs

policies.policy_description: NBFCs shall prepare their financial statements in accordance with Indian Accounting Standards (Ind AS) notified by the Government of India.
policies.objective: To ensure the transparency and accuracy of financial reporting

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Quoted current investments shall be valued at cost or market value whichever is lower.
Unquoted equity shares in the nature of current investments shall be valued at cost or breakup value, whichever is lower.
Unquoted preference shares in the nature of current investments shall be valued at cost or face value, whichever is lower.

policies.policy_description: The income recognition shall be based on recognised accounting principles.
policies.objective: To ensure the accuracy of financial reporting

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Income from dividend on shares of corporate bodies and units of mutual funds shall be taken into account on cash basis.

policies.policy_description: The asset classification norms as given below shall apply to applicable NBFCs.
policies.objective: To ensure the financial stability of NBFCs

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Assets shall be classified into Standard assets, Sub-standard assets, Doubtful assets, and Loss assets.

policies.policy_description: Framework for Prudential Regulation of NBFCs
policies.objective: To ensure the stability and soundness of the financial system by regulating the activities of NBFCs

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definition of Non-Performing Assets (NPAs) for applicable NBFCs
subpolicies.control: The extant NPA classification norm stands changed to the overdue period of more than 90 days for applicable NBFCs.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Timeline for adherence to the 90-day NPA norm by applicable NBFCs
subpolicies.control: NPA Norms Timeline >150 days overdue By March 31, 2024 >120 days overdue By March 31, 2025 > 90 days By March 31, 2026

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instructions for classifying borrower accounts as Special Mention Accounts (SMAs)
subpolicies.control: NBFCs shall recognize incipient stress in loan accounts, immediately on default, by classifying such assets as SMA as per the categories specified below.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Conditions for upgrading NPA accounts to standard asset category
subpolicies.control: Loan accounts classified as NPAs may be upgraded as ‘standard’ asset only if entire arrears of interest and principal are paid by the borrower.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instructions for consumer education on SMA/NPA classification and upgradation
subpolicies.control: NBFCs shall place consumer education literature on their websites, explaining with examples, the concepts of date of overdue, SMA and NPA classification and upgradation.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instructions for provisioning requirements for NBFCs
subpolicies.control: NBFC shall make provision against sub-standard assets, doubtful assets and loss assets as provided hereunder.

policies.policy_description: Framework for Prudential Regulation of NBFCs
policies.objective: To ensure prudent and sound banking practices by NBFCs

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Notionally compute the depreciated value of assets at 20% per annum on a straight-line method

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Additional Provision for Hire Purchase and Leased Assets
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Make additional provision as per the schedule below
subpolicies.control: Schedule: (i) Nil, (ii) 10%, (iii) 40%, (iv) 70%, (v) 100%

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Make provision for standard assets at 0.25% of outstanding
Follow instructions in paragraph 3 of Annex III and guidelines issued to banks on deferment of date of commencement of commercial operations (DCCO) for projects in commercial real estate (CRE) sector

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Prudential Framework for Resolution of Stressed Assets
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Follow instructions issued vide circular ‘The Prudential Framework for Resolution of Stressed Assets’ dated June 07, 2019, as amended from time to time

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Framework for Compromise Settlements and Technical Write-offs
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Comply with instructions contained in circular ‘Framework for Compromise Settlements and Technical Write-offs’ dated June 08, 2023, as amended from time to time

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Guidelines for Relief Measures by NBFCs in areas affected by Natural Calamities
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Follow guidelines issued vide ‘Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Relief Measures by Banks in Areas affected by Natural Calamities) Directions 2018 - SCBs’ dated October 17, 2018, as amended from time to time
Identify non-cooperative borrowers and make higher/accelerated provisioning as mentioned in paragraph 1.2.1 of Annex IV
Follow norms set forth in Annex III for restructuring of advances by non-deposit taking NBFCs with asset size less than ₹500 crore

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Flexible Structuring of Long-Term Project Loans to Infrastructure and Core Industries
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Follow norms set forth in Annex V for flexible structuring of long-term project loans to infrastructure and core industries by non-deposit taking NBFCs with asset size less than ₹500 crore

policies.policy_description: Non-deposit taking NBFCs with asset size less than ₹500 crore are allowed to refinance any existing infrastructure and other project loans by way of take-out financing, without a pre-determined agreement with other lenders and fix a longer repayment period.
policies.scope: Non-deposit taking NBFCs with asset size less than ₹500 crore
policies.objective: To provide flexibility to non-deposit taking NBFCs in refinancing project loans

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Such loans shall be 'standard' in the books of the existing lenders, and shall have not been restructured in the past.
Such loans shall be substantially taken over (more than 50 percent of the outstanding loan by value) from the existing financing lenders.
The repayment period shall be fixed by taking into account the life cycle of the project and cash flows from the project.

policies.policy_title: Early Recognition of Financial Distress, Prompt Steps for Resolution and Fair Recovery for Lenders
policies.policy_description: Framework for Revitalizing Distressed Assets in the Economy (Framework) as provided in Annex IV shall apply to non-deposit taking NBFCs with asset size less than ₹500 crore.
policies.scope: Non-deposit taking NBFCs with asset size less than ₹500 crore
policies.objective: To provide a framework for recognizing and resolving financial distress in non-deposit taking NBFCs

policies.policy_title: Guidelines on Liquidity Risk Management Framework
policies.policy_description: NBFCs having an asset size of ₹100 crore and above, as per their last audited balance sheet, shall adhere to the set of liquidity risk management guidelines as detailed in Annex VI of these Directions.
policies.scope: NBFCs having an asset size of ₹100 crore and above
policies.objective: To provide a framework for managing liquidity risk in NBFCs

policies.policy_title: Disclosures in Financial Statements - Notes to Accounts
policies.policy_description: NBFCs are required to make disclosures in their financial statements in accordance with the guidelines in these Directions, applicable accounting standards, laws and regulations.
policies.objective: To provide transparency in financial reporting by NBFCs

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall separately disclose in its balance sheet the provisions made as per these Directions without netting them from the income or against the value of assets.

policies.policy_description: The Board of Directors of NBFCs granting/intending to grant demand/call loans shall frame a policy for the company and implement the same.
policies.objective: To provide guidelines for managing demand/call loans in NBFCs

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: A cut-off date within which the repayment of demand or call loan shall be demanded or called up.
The rate of interest which shall be payable on such loans, and interest on such loans, as stipulated shall be payable either at monthly or quarterly basis.

policies.policy_description: The Board of Directors of NBFCs shall frame investment policy for the company and shall implement the same.
policies.objective: To provide guidelines for investments in NBFCs

policies.policy_description: The investment policy outlines the guidelines for current and long-term investments by the company.
policies.objective: To ensure prudent investment decisions that align with the company's goals and risk tolerance.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Investments shall be classified into current and long-term at the time of making each investment.
Transfers between current and long-term investments shall only occur on a half-yearly basis, with approval from the Board, and at book value or market value, whichever is lower.
Depreciation shall be fully provided for, and appreciation ignored, when transferring investments between classes.

policies.policy_description: The policy outlines the guidelines for preparing the balance sheet and profit and loss account of the company.
policies.objective: To ensure accurate and timely financial reporting.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The balance sheet shall be prepared as on March 31 every year, unless an extension is granted by the Reserve Bank.
Prior approval from the Reserve Bank is required to extend the date of the balance sheet beyond March 31.

policies.policy_description: The policy outlines the guidelines for appending the schedule to the balance sheet.
policies.objective: To ensure compliance with regulatory requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The schedule shall be appended to the balance sheet as prescribed under the Companies Act, 2013.

section_info.title: Chapter V Regulatory Restrictions and Limits

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: An NBFC which is held by an NOFHC shall not have any exposure (credit and investments including investments in the equity/debt capital instruments) to the Promoters/Promoter Group entities or individuals associated with the Promoter Group or the NOFHC.
policies.objective: To prevent concentration of credit and investments in NBFCs held by an NOFHC

policies.policy_description: NBFCs shall comply with the guidelines to declare dividends.
policies.objective: To ensure that NBFCs declare dividends in a prudent manner

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall meet the minimum prudential requirements to declare dividend.
subpolicies.control: Minimum capital requirements, net NPA ratio, and compliance with RBI regulations

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs can declare dividend up to a dividend payout ratio of 50 percent or subject to a cap of 10 percent if they do not meet the eligibility criteria.
subpolicies.control: Dividend payout ratio

policies.policy_description: There shall be a ceiling of ₹1 crore per borrower for financing subscription to Initial Public Offer (IPO).
policies.objective: To prevent excessive funding for IPOs

policies.policy_description: No NBFC shall lend against its own shares.
policies.objective: To prevent self-financing and maintain prudence in lending

policies.policy_description: NBFCs shall maintain a Loan-to-Value (LTV) ratio of 50 percent for loans granted against the collateral of shares.
policies.scope: All NBFCs with asset size of ₹100 crore and above
policies.objective: To ensure that NBFCs maintain a prudent LTV ratio for share-based lending

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Acceptable Collateral for Loans against Shares
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall accept only Group 1 securities as collateral for loans of value more than ₹5 lakh.
subpolicies.control: Group 1 securities

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall report on-line to stock exchanges on a quarterly basis, information on the shares pledged in their favour.
subpolicies.control: Quarterly reporting to stock exchanges

policies.policy_title: Loans against Security of Single Product - Gold Jewellery
policies.policy_description: NBFCs shall maintain a Loan-to-Value (LTV) Ratio not exceeding 75 percent for loans granted against the collateral of gold jewellery.
policies.objective: To ensure that NBFCs maintain a prudent LTV ratio for gold jewellery-based lending

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Verification of Ownership of Gold Jewellery
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall keep a record of the verification of ownership of the jewellery.
subpolicies.control: Record of ownership verification

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Standardization of Value of Gold accepted as Collateral
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The gold jewellery accepted as collateral by the NBFC shall be valued by taking into account the preceding 30 days’ average of the closing price of 22 carat gold.
subpolicies.control: Valuation method for gold jewellery

policies.policy_description: Regulatory restrictions and limits for NBFCs lending against collateral of gold jewellery
policies.scope: NBFCs lending against collateral of gold jewellery
policies.objective: To ensure the safety and security of gold jewellery accepted as collateral and to provide convenience to borrowers

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Conditions for auctioning pledged ornaments
subpolicies.control: (i) The first auction has failed. (ii) The NBFC shall ensure that all other requirements of the extant directions regarding auction are met.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The reserve price for pledged ornaments shall not be less than 85 percent of the previous 30 day average closing price of 22 carat gold as declared by the Bombay Bullion Association Ltd. (BBA) or the historical spot gold price data publicly disseminated by a commodity exchange regulated by the Forward Markets Commission and value of the jewellery of lower purity in terms of carats shall be proportionately reduced.

NBFCs shall disclose in their annual reports the details of the auctions conducted during the financial year including the number of loan accounts, outstanding amounts, value fetched and whether any of its sister concerns participated in the auction.

NBFCs shall ensure that necessary infrastructure and facilities are put in place, including safe deposit vault and appropriate security measures for operating the vault, in each of its branches where gold jewellery is accepted as collateral.

NBFCs which are in the business of lending against collateral of gold jewellery shall obtain prior approval of the Reserve Bank to open branches exceeding 1,000. No new branches shall be allowed to be opened without suitable arrangements for security and for storage of gold jewellery.

section_info.title: Chapter VI Governance Guidelines

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_title: Loans to directors, senior officers and relatives of directors
policies.policy_description: NBFCs shall have a Board approved policy on grant of loans to directors, senior officers and relatives of directors and to entities where directors or their relatives have major shareholding.
policies.objective: To ensure that loans are granted in accordance with the Board's approval and within a threshold beyond which loans shall be reported to the Board.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The Board approved policy shall include a threshold beyond which loans to abovementioned persons shall be reported to the Board.

policies.policy_title: Appointment of Statutory Central Auditors/Statutory Auditors
policies.policy_description: NBFCs shall adhere to the instructions contained in circular titled ‘Guidelines for Appointment of Statutory Central Auditors (SCAs)/ Statutory Auditors (SAs) of Commercial Banks (excluding RRBs), UCBs and NBFCs (including HFCs)’ dated April 27, 2021, as amended from time to time.
policies.objective: To ensure that Statutory Central Auditors/Statutory Auditors are appointed in accordance with the Reserve Bank's guidelines.

policies.policy_description: An NBFC shall require prior written permission of the Reserve Bank for the following: (i) Any takeover or acquisition of control of the NBFC, may or may not result in change of management;
policies.objective: To ensure that changes in control or ownership are made in accordance with the Reserve Bank's guidelines.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall submit an application, in the company’s letter head, for obtaining prior approval of the Reserve Bank, along with the following documents: (i) information about the proposed directors/shareholders as per Annex XII; (ii) sources of funds of the proposed shareholders acquiring the shares in the NBFC;
subpolicies.control: The Regional Office of the Department of Supervision of the Reserve Bank in whose jurisdiction the Registered Office of the NBFC is located.

policies.policy_title: Information with respect to change of address, directors, auditors, etc.
policies.policy_description: NBFCs shall communicate, not later than one month from the occurrence of any change in: (i) the complete postal address, telephone number/s and fax number/s of the registered/ corporate office;
policies.objective: To ensure that changes are reported to the Reserve Bank in a timely manner.

section_info.title: Chapter VII Fair Practices Code

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: Chapter VII Fair Practices Code for NBFCs having customer interface.
policies.objective: To ensure fair practices in lending and borrowing activities

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Applications for Loans and their Processing
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Guidelines for processing loan applications, including communication with borrowers.
subpolicies.control: 45.1 Applications for loans and their processing

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Guidelines for conveying loan terms and conditions to borrowers.
subpolicies.control: 45.2 Loan appraisal and terms/conditions

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Guidelines for charging penal charges on loans, including disclosure and communication with borrowers.
subpolicies.control: 45.3 15Penal charges in loan accounts

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Disbursement of Loans including Changes in Terms and Conditions
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Guidelines for disbursement of loans, including notice to borrowers of changes in terms and conditions.
subpolicies.control: 45.4 Disbursement of loans including changes in terms and conditions

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Responsible Lending Conduct - Release of Movable/Immovable Property Documents on Repayment/Settlement of Personal Loans
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Guidelines for releasing movable/immovable property documents upon repayment/settlement of personal loans.
subpolicies.control: 45.5 16Responsible Lending Conduct - Release of movable/immovable property documents on repayment/settlement of personal loans

policies.policy_description: Framework for fair practices in NBFCs
policies.objective: To ensure fair treatment of borrowers and provide transparency in loan agreements

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Release of Original Movable/Immovable Property Documents
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall assist borrowers in obtaining duplicate/certified copies of documents and bear associated costs, with an additional 30-day period for completion.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Reset of Floating Interest Rate on Equated Monthly Instalments (EMI) based Personal Loans
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall provide clear communication to borrowers about possible changes in EMI and/or tenor, offer option to switch to fixed rate, and disclose charges for switching.
NBFCs shall refrain from interference in borrower affairs, provide transparent contractual terms for loan transfer, and ensure staff training for customer interaction.
The Board of Directors shall establish a grievance redressal mechanism and review compliance with the Fair Practices Code.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Regulation of Excessive Interest Charged by NBFCs
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall adopt an interest rate model, disclose rates to borrowers, and update information on their website.

policies.policy_description: Code for fair practices by NBFCs
policies.objective: To ensure fair and transparent lending practices

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Levying of Excessive Interest and Charges
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Guidelines for determining interest rates and processing charges
subpolicies.control: Boards of NBFCs shall lay out appropriate internal principles and procedures

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Repossession of Vehicles Financed by NBFCs
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Procedures for repossession, notice period, and sale/auction of vehicles
subpolicies.control: NBFCs must have a built-in re-possession clause in the contract/loan agreement

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Lending against Collateral of Gold Jewellery
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Guidelines for lending against gold jewellery, including KYC, assaying, and auction procedures
subpolicies.control: NBFCs shall put in place Board-approved policy for lending against gold

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Loan Facilities to Physically/Visually Challenged Applicants
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Non-discrimination and assistance for physically/visually challenged applicants
subpolicies.control: NBFCs shall not discriminate in extending products and facilities

section_info.title: Chapter VIII Miscellaneous Instructions

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_title: Opening of Branch/Subsidiary/Joint Venture/Representative Office or Undertaking Investment Abroad by NBFCs
policies.policy_description: The instructions in the following paragraphs are in addition to those prescribed by Foreign Exchange Department (FED) of the Reserve Bank for overseas investment.
policies.objective: To regulate the opening of branches, subsidiaries, joint ventures, representative offices or undertaking investments abroad by NBFCs

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The aggregate overseas investment shall not exceed 100 percent of the NOF. The overseas investment in a single entity, including its stepdown subsidiaries, by way of equity or fund-based commitment shall not be more than 15 percent of the NBFC's owned funds.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Specific Conditions for Opening of Branch Abroad
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs which have already set up branch(es) abroad for undertaking financial business shall be allowed to continue to operate them subject to complying with these directions, as applicable.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Specific Conditions for Opening of Subsidiary Abroad
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The parent NBFC shall not be permitted to extend implicit or explicit guarantee to or on behalf of such subsidiaries; No request for letter of comfort in favour of the subsidiary abroad from any institution in India shall be permitted.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Specific Conditions for Opening of Joint Ventures Abroad
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Investments abroad, other than in subsidiaries shall also be governed by same guidelines as those applicable to subsidiaries.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Specific Conditions for Opening of Representative Offices Abroad
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The representative office can be set up abroad for the purpose of liaison work, undertaking market study and research but not undertaking any activity which involves outlay of funds.

policies.policy_title: Expansion of Activities of NBFCs through Automatic Route
policies.policy_description: NBFCs with Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) under the automatic route shall be permitted to undertake only those activities which are permissible under the automatic route.
policies.objective: To regulate the expansion of activities of NBFCs through the automatic route

policies.policy_title: Managing Risks and Code of Conduct in Outsourcing of Financial Services by NBFCs
policies.policy_description: NBFCs shall conduct a self-assessment of their existing outsourcing arrangements and bring these in line with the instructions as provided at Annex XIII.
policies.objective: To manage risks and ensure compliance with code of conduct in outsourcing of financial services by NBFCs

policies.policy_description: NBFCs shall comply with the instructions contained in circular on ‘Guidelines on Digital Lending’ dated September 02, 2022, as amended from time to time.
policies.objective: To regulate digital lending by NBFCs

policies.policy_title: Guidelines on Default Loss Guarantee (DLG) in Digital Lending
policies.policy_description: NBFCs shall comply with the instructions contained in circular on ‘Guidelines on Default Loss Guarantee (DLG) in Digital Lending’ dated June 08, 2023, as amended from time to time.
policies.objective: To regulate default loss guarantee in digital lending by NBFCs

policies.policy_title: Loans Sourced by NBFCs over Digital Lending Platforms: Adherence to Fair Practices Code and Outsourcing Guidelines
policies.policy_description: Vide circular
policies.objective: To regulate loans sourced by NBFCs over digital lending platforms

policies.policy_description: Guidelines for NBFCs to engage digital lending platforms as agents.
policies.objective: To ensure transparency and compliance with regulatory instructions in digital lending transactions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs must disclose the names of digital lending platforms engaged as agents on their website.
subpolicies.control: (i) Names of digital lending platforms engaged as agents shall be disclosed on the website of NBFCs.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Digital lending platforms must disclose upfront to customers the name of the NBFC on whose behalf they are interacting with them.
subpolicies.control: (ii) Digital lending platforms engaged as agents shall be directed to disclose upfront to the customer, the name of the NBFC on whose behalf they are interacting with him.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The sanction letter must be issued to the borrower immediately after sanction but before execution of the loan agreement.
subpolicies.control: (iii) Immediately after sanction but before execution of the loan agreement, the sanction letter shall be issued to the borrower on the letter head of the NBFC concerned.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: A copy of the loan agreement along with a copy each of all enclosures quoted in the loan agreement must be furnished to all borrowers at the time of sanction/disbursement of loans.
subpolicies.control: (iv) A copy of the loan agreement along with a copy each of all enclosures quoted in the loan agreement shall be furnished to all borrowers at the time of sanction/ disbursement of loans.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Effective oversight and monitoring must be ensured over digital lending platforms engaged by NBFCs.
subpolicies.control: (v) Effective oversight and monitoring shall be ensured over the digital lending platforms engaged by the NBFCs.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Adequate efforts must be made towards creation of awareness about the grievance redressal mechanism.
subpolicies.control: (vi) Adequate efforts shall be made towards creation of awareness about the grievance redressal mechanism.

policies.policy_description: Guidelines for NBFCs to participate in CDS market as users.
policies.objective: To ensure compliance with regulatory instructions and guidelines on CDS transactions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall only participate in the CDS market as users, buying credit protection to hedge their credit risk on corporate bonds they hold.
subpolicies.control: NBFCs shall buy credit protection only to hedge their credit risk on corporate bonds they hold.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs must ensure that the guidelines enclosed, including operational requirements for CDS as provided in Annex XIV, are fulfilled.
subpolicies.control: Apart from complying with all the provisions above, NBFCs shall, as users, also ensure that the guidelines enclosed including operational requirements for CDS as provided in Annex XIV, are fulfilled by them.

policies.policy_description: Guidelines for NBFCs to participate in currency futures exchanges.
policies.objective: To ensure compliance with regulatory instructions and guidelines on currency futures transactions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall participate in designated currency futures exchanges recognized by SEBI as clients, subject to Bank's (Foreign Exchange Department) guidelines in the matter.
subpolicies.control: NBFCs shall participate in the designated currency futures exchanges recognized by SEBI as clients, subject to Bank's (Foreign Exchange Department) guidelines in the matter.

policies.policy_description: Guidelines for NBFCs to participate in interest rate futures exchanges.
policies.objective: To ensure compliance with regulatory instructions and guidelines on interest rate futures transactions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Participation in Interest Rate Futures Exchanges
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs can participate in designated interest rate futures (IRF) exchanges recognized by SEBI, as clients, subject to adherence to instructions contained in ‘Rupee Interest Rate Derivatives (Reserve Bank) Directions, 2019’ dated June 26, 2019, as amended from time to time.
subpolicies.control: NBFCs can participate in the designated interest rate futures (IRF) exchanges recognized by SEBI, as clients, subject to adherence to instructions contained in ‘Rupee Interest Rate Derivatives (Reserve Bank) Directions, 2019’ dated June 26, 2019, as amended from time to time.

policies.policy_description: Guidelines for NBFCs to undertake transactions in government securities.
policies.objective: To ensure compliance with regulatory instructions and guidelines on government securities transactions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall undertake transactions in government securities through its gilt account or its demat account or any other account, as permitted by the Reserve Bank.
subpolicies.control: NBFCs shall undertake transactions in Government securities through its gilt account or its demat account or any other account, as permitted by the Reserve Bank.

policies.policy_title: Reporting Platform for Corporate Bond Transactions
policies.policy_description: Guidelines for NBFCs to report their secondary market OTC trades in corporate bonds.
policies.objective: To ensure compliance with regulatory instructions and guidelines on reporting of corporate bond transactions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs should report their secondary market OTC trades in corporate bonds within 15 minutes of the trade on any of the stock exchanges (NSE, BSE and MCX-SX).
subpolicies.control: NBFCs should report their secondary market OTC trades in corporate bonds within 15 minutes of the trade on any of the stock exchanges (NSE, BSE and MCX-SX).

policies.policy_title: Raising Money through Private Placement by NBFCs
policies.policy_description: Guidelines for NBFCs to raise money through private placement of non-convertible debentures.
policies.objective: To ensure compliance with regulatory instructions and guidelines on private placement of non-convertible debentures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall follow the guidelines on private placement of non-convertible debentures given in Annex XV.
subpolicies.control: NBFCs shall follow the guidelines on private placement of non-convertible debentures given in Annex XV.

policies.policy_description: Guidelines for NBFCs to enter into insurance business.
policies.objective: To ensure compliance with regulatory instructions and guidelines on entry into insurance business.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Application for Entry into Insurance Business
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall make an application along with necessary particulars duly certified by their statutory auditors to the Regional Office of Department of Supervision of the Reserve Bank in whose jurisdiction the registered office of the NBFCs is situated.
subpolicies.control: (i) For entry into insurance business, NBFCs shall make an application along with necessary particulars duly certified by their statutory auditors to the Regional Office of Department of Supervision of the Reserve Bank in whose jurisdiction the registered office of the NBFCs is situated.

policies.policy_description: Guidelines for NBFCs to issue co-branded credit cards with scheduled commercial banks.
policies.objective: To ensure compliance with regulatory instructions and guidelines on issuance of co-branded credit cards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Eligibility Criteria for Issuance of Co-branded Credit Cards
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs fulfilling the minimum eligibility requirements and adhering to certain stipulations are eligible to apply.
subpolicies.control: NBFCs fulfilling the minimum eligibility requirements and adhering to certain stipulations are eligible to apply.

policies.policy_description: General guidelines for NBFCs on miscellaneous matters.
policies.objective: To ensure compliance with regulatory instructions and guidelines on miscellaneous matters.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Effective oversight and monitoring must be ensured over NBFCs to ensure compliance with regulatory instructions and guidelines.
subpolicies.control: Effective oversight and monitoring shall be ensured over the NBFCs.

policies.policy_description: Deposit accepting NBFCs shall not undertake such activity.
policies.objective: To ensure compliance with Reserve Bank regulations

policies.policy_title: Point of Presence Services under Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority
policies.policy_description: NBFC-BL shall not undertake Point of Presence (PoP) services for National Pension System (NPS) under Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority.
policies.objective: To ensure compliance with Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority regulations

policies.policy_title: Provision of Safe Deposit Locker Facility
policies.policy_description: Providing safe deposit locker facility is a fee-based service and shall not be reckoned as part of the financial business carried out by NBFCs.
policies.objective: To ensure transparency in financial services

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs offering safe deposit locker facility or intending to offer it, shall disclose to their customers that the activity is not regulated by the Reserve Bank.
subpolicies.control: Customer Disclosure

policies.policy_description: Non-individual borrowers enjoying aggregate exposure of ₹5 crore and above from banks and financial institutions shall be required to obtain LEI codes as per the timeline given below.
policies.objective: To improve risk management and financial data systems

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Borrowers can obtain LEI from any of the Local Operating Units (LOUs) accredited by the Global Legal Entity Identifier Foundation (GLEIF).
subpolicies.control: LEI Code Issuance

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: After obtaining LEI code, NBFCs shall also ensure that borrowers renew the codes as per GLEIF guidelines.
subpolicies.control: LEI Code Renewal

policies.policy_title: Submission of Data to Credit Information Companies (CICs)
policies.policy_description: All NBFCs (except those which are purely into investment activities without any customer interface) shall become member of all CICs and submit data (including historical data) to them.
policies.objective: To ensure compliance with Credit Information Companies regulations

policies.policy_title: Data Format for Furnishing of Credit Information to CICs and other Regulatory Measures
policies.policy_description: All NBFCs (except those which are purely into investment activities without any customer interface) shall comply with the instructions contained in the Bank’s circular DBOD.No.CID.BC.127/20.16.056/2013-14 dated June 27, 2014 and subsequent instructions on the subject.
policies.objective: To ensure compliance with Credit Information Companies regulations

policies.policy_title: Filing of Records of Mortgages with the Central Registry
policies.policy_description: NBFCs shall file and register the records of equitable mortgages created in their favour on or after March 31, 2011 with the Central Registry of Securitisation Asset Reconstruction and Security Interest of India (Central Registry) and shall also register the records with the Central Registry as and when equitable mortgages are created in their favour.
policies.objective: To ensure compliance with Securitisation and Reconstruction of Financial Assets and Enforcement of Security Interest regulations

policies.policy_title: Display of Information - Secured assets possessed under the SARFAESI Act, 2002
policies.policy_description: NBFCs which are secured creditors as per the Securitisation and Reconstruction of Financial Assets and Enforcement of Security Interest (SARFAESI) Act, 2002, shall display information in respect of the borrowers whose secured assets have been taken into possession by them under the said Act.
policies.objective: To ensure transparency in financial services

policies.policy_title: Technical Specifications for all participants of the Account Aggregator ecosystem
policies.policy_description: The NBFC-AA consolidates financial information, as defined in paragraph 3(1) ix of Master Direction- Non-Banking Financial Company - Account Aggregator (Reserve Bank) Directions, 2016, dated September 02, 2016 of a customer held with different financial entities, spread across financial sector regulators adopting different IT systems and interfaces.
policies.objective: To ensure compliance with Reserve Bank regulations

policies.policy_description: NBFCs shall stipulate that builders/developers/owners/companies disclose the name(s) of the entity to which the property is mortgaged and provide NOC/permission for sale of flats/property, if required.
policies.objective: To ensure transparency in housing finance transactions

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The builder/developer/owner/company shall disclose the name(s) of the entity to which the property is mortgaged.
subpolicies.control: NBFCs shall ensure compliance with this stipulation and funds shall not be released unless the builder/developer/owner/company fulfils this requirement.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: NO Objection Certificate (NOC) for Sale of Flats/Property
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The builder/developer/owner/company shall indicate in pamphlets/brochures that they would provide NOC/permission for sale of flats/property, if required.
subpolicies.control: NBFCs shall ensure compliance with this stipulation and funds shall not be released unless the builder/developer/owner/company fulfils this requirement.

policies.policy_title: NBFCs not to be partners in partnership firms
policies.policy_description: No NBFC shall contribute to the capital of a partnership firm or become a partner of such firm.
policies.objective: To ensure that NBFCs do not engage in activities that may compromise their independence and impartiality

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Prohibition on Contribution to Partnership Firm Capital
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: No NBFC shall contribute to the capital of a partnership firm.
subpolicies.control: NBFCs which had already contributed to the capital of a partnership firm or are a partner of a partnership firm shall seek early retirement from the partnership firm.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Prohibition on Becoming a Partner in Partnership Firm
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: No NBFC shall become a partner of a partnership firm.
subpolicies.control: NBFCs which had already contributed to the capital of a partnership firm or are a partner of a partnership firm shall seek early retirement from the partnership firm.

policies.policy_title: Ratings of financial products of NBFCs
policies.policy_description: NBFCs with asset size of ₹100 crore and above shall furnish information about downgrading/upgrading of assigned rating of any financial product issued by them, within fifteen days of such a change in rating.
policies.scope: All NBFCs with asset size of ₹100 crore and above
policies.objective: To ensure transparency and accountability in the rating process

policies.policy_title: Non-Reckoning of Fixed Deposits with banks as Financial Assets
policies.policy_description: Investments in fixed deposits shall not be treated as financial assets, and receipt of interest income on fixed deposits with banks shall not be treated as income from financial assets.
policies.objective: To ensure that NBFCs do not engage in activities that may compromise their financial stability

policies.policy_description: NBFCs shall take proactive steps for increasing the use of electronic payment systems, elimination of post-dated cheques and gradual phase-out of cheques in their day-to-day business transactions.
policies.objective: To reduce costs and improve efficiency in financial transactions

policies.policy_title: Migration of Post-Dated Cheques (PDCs)/ Equated Monthly Instalment (EMI) Cheques to National Automated Clearing House(NACH)(Debit)
policies.policy_description: NBFCs shall not accept fresh/ additional PDCs or EMI cheques from customers, and shall use NACH (Debit) mandates instead.
policies.objective: To reduce costs and improve efficiency in financial transactions

policies.policy_title: Unsolicited Commercial Communications - National Do Not Call Registry
policies.policy_description: NBFCs shall not engage telemarketers who do not have any valid registration certificate from DoT, Government of India, as telemarketers.
policies.objective: To protect customers from unsolicited commercial communications

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall engage only those telemarketers who are registered in terms of the guidelines issued by TRAI, from time to time.
subpolicies.control: NBFCs shall furnish the list of Telemarketers (DSAs/DMAs) engaged by them along with the registered telephone numbers being used by them for making telemarketing calls to TRAI.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall ensure that all agents presently engaged by them register themselves with DoT as telemarketers.
subpolicies.control: NBFCs shall furnish the list of Telemarketers (DSAs/DMAs) engaged by them along with the registered telephone numbers being used by them for making telemarketing calls to TRAI.

policies.policy_title: Attempt to defraud using fake bank guarantee - Modus Operandi
policies.policy_description: NBFCs shall take notice of the above facts in order to exercise due caution while handling such cases.
policies.objective: To prevent fraud and ensure financial stability

policies.policy_title: Disbursal of loan amount in cash
policies.policy_description: NBFCs shall ensure compliance with the requirements under sections 269SS and 269T of the Income Tax Act, 1961, as amended from time to time.
policies.objective: To ensure compliance with tax laws and regulations

policies.policy_title: Rounding off of transactions to the Nearest Rupee
policies.policy_description: All transactions of NBFCs, including payment of interest on deposits/ charging of interest on advances, shall be rounded off to the nearest rupee.
policies.objective: To reduce errors and improve efficiency in financial transactions

section_info.title: Section III Regulations applicable for NBFC-ML

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_title: Section III Regulations applicable for NBFC-ML
policies.policy_description: Regulatory instructions specified in Section III shall be applicable to NBFC-ML.
policies.objective: To specify the regulatory instructions applicable to NBFC-ML

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Regulatory instructions specified in Section III shall be applicable to NBFC-ML.
Regulatory instructions applicable to NBFC-BL as specified in Section II shall also be applicable to NBFC-ML, unless stated otherwise.

section_info.title: Chapter IX Prudential Regulations

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: NBFCs shall maintain a minimum capital ratio consisting of Tier 1 and Tier 2 capital which shall not be less than 15 percent of its aggregate risk weighted assets on balance sheet and of risk adjusted value of off-balance sheet items.
policies.objective: To ensure the availability of adequate capital to support all risks in business

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Tier 1 capital in respect of NBFC (except NBFC-MFI and NBFC primarily engaged in lending against gold jewellery), at any point of time, shall not be less than 10 percent.
NBFCs are permitted to augment their capital funds by issue of Perpetual Debt Instruments (PDI) in accordance with the guidelines contained in the Annex XX.

policies.policy_description: NBFCs are required to make a thorough internal assessment of the need for capital, commensurate with the risks in their business.
policies.objective: To ensure availability of adequate capital to support all risks in business and encourage NBFCs to develop and use better internal risk management techniques

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Internal capital assessment shall factor in credit risk, market risk, operational risk and all other residual risks as per methodology to be determined internally.

policies.policy_title: Treatment of On-Balance Sheet Assets for Capital Ratio
policies.policy_description: The degree of credit risk expressed as percentage weightages have been assigned to balance sheet assets.
policies.objective: To ensure the availability of adequate capital to support all risks in business

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The risk weighted assets shall be calculated as the weighted aggregate of funded items.

policies.policy_title: Treatment of Off-Balance Sheet Items for Capital Ratio
policies.policy_description: NBFCs shall calculate the total risk adjusted value of off-balance sheet items.
policies.objective: To ensure the availability of adequate capital to support all risks in business

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFC shall calculate the total risk adjusted value of off-balance sheet items.

policies.policy_description: Risk weighted off-balance sheet credit exposure as the sum of the risk-weighted amount of the market related and non-market related off-balance sheet items.
policies.objective: To ensure that NBFCs maintain a prudent level of credit exposure in their off-balance sheet transactions

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Risk Weighted Off-Balance Sheet Credit Exposure
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The risk-weighted amount of an off-balance sheet item that gives rise to credit exposure shall be calculated by means of a two-step process.

The credit equivalent amount in relation to a non-market related off-balance sheet item shall be determined by multiplying the contracted amount of that particular transaction by the relevant credit conversion factor (CCF).
The credit risk on market related off-balance sheet items is the cost to an NBFC of replacing the cash flow specified by the contract in the event of counterparty default.

The credit equivalent amount of a market related off-balance sheet transaction calculated using the current exposure method is the sum of (i) current exposure and (ii) potential future exposure of the contract.

policies.policy_description: Framework for Prudential Regulations
policies.objective: To ensure the stability and soundness of the financial system

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Calculation of residual maturity for interest rate contracts
Calculation of potential future exposure for single currency floating/floating interest rate swaps
Use of effective notional amount instead of stated notional amount for calculating potential future exposure
Recognition and calculation of bilateral netting contract for regulatory capital purposes

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Regulatory treatment of DTA and DTL for computation of capital

policies.scope: All NBFCs (except microfinance loans of NBFC-MFIs)
policies.objective: To ensure the stability and soundness of NBFCs by regulating their asset classification, provisioning, and capital adequacy.

Norms for classifying assets into Standard, Sub-standard, Doubtful, and Loss categories.

Sub-categories of SMA based on the period of overdue payment.
Regulations for upgrading NPA accounts to Standard category after repayment of arrears.
Guidelines for NBFCs to educate borrowers about SMA and NPA concepts.

policies.policy_description: NBFC shall make provisions for standard assets at 0.40 percent of the outstanding, which shall not be reckoned for arriving at net NPAs.
policies.objective: To ensure that NBFCs maintain a sufficient provision for standard assets

policies.policy_title: Guidelines on Maintenance of Liquidity Coverage Ratio (LCR)
policies.policy_description: NBFCs shall adhere to the guidelines on LCR including disclosure standards as provided in Annex XXI.
policies.objective: To ensure that NBFCs maintain a sufficient liquidity buffer

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: LCR Requirements for Non-Deposit Taking NBFCs with Asset Size of ₹10,000 Crore and Above
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall maintain a minimum LCR of 100 percent of total net cash outflows over the next 30 calendar days.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: LCR Requirements for Non-Deposit Taking NBFCs with Asset Size of ₹5,000 Crore to ₹10,000 Crore
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall maintain a minimum LCR of 30 percent of total net cash outflows over the next 30 calendar days.
Core Investment Companies, Type I NBFCs, NOFHCs and SPDs are exempt from the applicability of LCR norms.

policies.policy_title: Disclosure in Financial Statement - Notes to Accounts
policies.policy_description: NBFCs shall put up to the Board of Directors, at regular intervals, as may be prescribed by the Board in this regard, the following: ...
policies.objective: To ensure that NBFCs provide transparent and accurate financial information

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Disclosure Requirements for Progress Made in Putting in Place a Progressive Risk Management System
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall put up to the Board of Directors, at regular intervals, as may be prescribed by the Board in this regard, the progress made in putting in place a progressive risk management system.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Disclosure Requirements for Conformity with Corporate Governance Standards
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall put up to the Board of Directors, at regular intervals, as may be prescribed by the Board in this regard, conformity with corporate governance standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Disclosure Requirements for Annual Financial Statements
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall disclose the following information in their Annual Financial Statements: ...

section_info.title: Chapter X Regulatory Restrictions and Limits

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: Regulatory restrictions on credit and investment exposure for NBFCs.
policies.objective: To ensure prudent risk management and maintain financial stability

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFC shall not have exposure exceeding 25% of its Tier 1 capital to a single party, and 40% to a single group of parties.
NBFC-IFC shall not have exposure exceeding 30% of its Tier 1 capital to a single party, and 50% to a single group of parties.
NBFCs may exceed the exposure norms by 5% for any single party and by 10% for a single group of parties, if the additional exposure is on account of infrastructure loan and/or investment.

policies.policy_description: Regulatory restrictions on exposure to sensitive sectors such as capital market and commercial real estate.
policies.objective: To ensure prudent risk management and maintain financial stability

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall fix board-approved internal limits for SSE separately for capital market and commercial real estate exposures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Sub-limits within Commercial Real Estate Exposure Ceiling
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: A sub-limit within the commercial real estate exposure ceiling shall be fixed internally for financing land acquisition.

policies.policy_description: Regulatory restrictions on loans and advances to directors, senior officers, and real estate sector.
policies.objective: To ensure prudent risk management and maintain financial stability

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall not grant loans and advances aggregating Rupees five crores and above to directors or their relatives without Board approval.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Loans and Advances to Senior Officers
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall report loans and advances sanctioned to senior officers to the Board, and no senior officer shall sanction credit facilities to a relative of that officer.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Loans and Advances to Real Estate Sector
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall ensure that borrowers have obtained prior permission from Government/local Government/statutory authorities for real estate projects, and disbursements shall be made only after the borrower has obtained requisite clearances.

section_info.title: Chapter XI Governance Guidelines

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_title: Constitution of Committees of the Board
policies.policy_description: Guidelines for constituting Audit Committee and Nomination and Remuneration Committee in NBFCs.
policies.objective: To ensure effective governance and risk management practices in NBFCs.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The Audit Committee shall consist of not less than three members of the Board of Directors, with powers, functions, and duties as laid down in section 177 of the Companies Act, 2013.
The Nomination and Remuneration Committee shall have the constitution, powers, functions, and duties as laid down in section 178 of the Companies Act, 2013.

policies.policy_description: Guidelines for appointing a Chief Risk Officer (CRO) in NBFCs with asset size of more than ₹5,000 crore.
policies.scope: NBFCs with asset size of more than ₹5,000 crore
policies.objective: To ensure effective risk management practices in NBFCs.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The CRO shall be a senior official with adequate professional qualification/experience in the area of risk management, appointed for a fixed tenure with approval of the Board.
The CRO shall have direct reporting lines to the MD and CEO/Risk Management Committee (RMC) of the Board, without any reporting relationship with business verticals or 'dual hatting'.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Involvement in Risk Identification and Mitigation
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The CRO shall be involved in the process of identification, measurement, and mitigation of risks, including vetting credit products from an angle of inherent and control risks.

policies.policy_description: Guidelines for ascertaining the 'fit and proper' criteria of directors in NBFCs.
policies.objective: To ensure that only suitable and competent individuals are appointed as directors in NBFCs.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The policy shall be put in place with the approval of the Board, including a declaration and undertaking from directors, a Deed of Covenant signed by directors, and quarterly statements on change of directors.

policies.policy_title: Guidelines on Compensation of Key Managerial Personnel (KMP) and Senior Management
policies.policy_description: Guidelines for formulating compensation policies for KMP and senior management in NBFCs.
policies.objective: To ensure that compensation packages are aligned with the interests of stakeholders and do not encourage excessive risk-taking behavior.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The Board shall constitute a Remuneration Committee to formulate compensation policies for KMP and senior management.
The policy shall include principles for fixed and variable pay structures, including malus/clawback provisions.

policies.policy_title: Framing of Internal Guidelines on Corporate Governance
policies.policy_description: Guidelines for framing internal guidelines on corporate governance in NBFCs.
policies.objective: To ensure effective implementation of corporate governance practices in NBFCs.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The internal guidelines shall enhance the scope of corporate governance practices in NBFCs, with approval of the Board.

policies.policy_description: without sacrificing the spirit underlying the guidelines in Chapter XI and it shall be published on the company's website, if any, for the information of various stakeholders.
policies.objective: To ensure compliance with governance guidelines

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: As defined in Section 2 (51) of Companies Act, 2013, as amended from time to time.
'Senior Management' are the same as defined in ‘Explanation’ to Section 178 of the Companies Act, 2013.

policies.objective: To allow non-deposit taking NBFCs to participate in interest rate futures market

policies.objective: To allow non-deposit taking NBFCs to participate in repo transactions in corporate debt securities

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Capital Adequacy Risk Weights for Credit Risk
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Risk weights for credit risk for assets that are the collateral for such transactions shall be as applicable to the issuer/counterparty under paragraphs 84 and 85 of these Directions.
subpolicies.control: Paragraphs 84 and 85 of ‘Repurchase Transactions (Repo) (Reserve Bank) Directions, 2018’

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Balances in the accounts viz. repo account, reverse repo account etc. shall be done in the relevant schedules similar to that of banks.
subpolicies.control: Paragraphs 84 and 85 of ‘Repurchase Transactions (Repo) (Reserve Bank) Directions, 2018’

policies.policy_title: Undertaking of Point of Presence (PoP) Services under Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority for National Pension System (NPS)
policies.policy_description: NBFCs which comply with the prescribed CRAR and made net profit in the preceding financial year be permitted to undertake PoP services under PFRDA for NPS after registration with PFRDA.
policies.objective: To allow NBFCs to undertake PoP services under PFRDA for NPS

policies.policy_title: Licensing as Authorised Dealer- Category II
policies.policy_description: Non-deposit taking NBFC-ICC shall be eligible for Authorized Dealer- Category II (AD-Cat II) licence, subject to meeting the following conditions.
policies.objective: To allow non-deposit taking NBFC-ICC to obtain AD-Cat II licence

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs offering such services shall have a ‘minimum investment grade rating’.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Board Approved Policy on Managing Risks and Handling Customer Grievances
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs offering such services shall put in place a board approved policy on managing the risks, including currency risk, if any, arising out of such activities and handling customer grievances arising out of such activities.

policies.policy_description: NBFCs fulfilling the criteria laid down under the circular on ‘Appointment of Internal Ombudsman by Non-Banking Financial Companies’ dated November 15, 2021 shall appoint the Internal Ombudsman and adhere to the corresponding guidelines.

policies.policy_description: Regulations for NBFCs to ensure prudence in lending and risk management.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Treatment of Provisions for Standard Assets
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Provisions for standard assets shall be treated in accordance with the prudential floor as prescribed under paragraph 2 of Annex II.
NBFCs with net worth of ₹250 crore or above are required to hold impairment allowances as required under Ind AS, subject to the prudential floor.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Definition of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The definition of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises shall be as per the circular on ‘Credit flow to Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises Sector’ dated July 2, 2020.
Loans for creation/acquisition of commercial real estate shall be treated as CRE exposure.
Loans to builders/developers for residential housing projects shall be treated as CRE-RH exposure, subject to certain conditions.
Housing loans having comparatively lower rates of interest in the first few years after which the rates are reset at higher rates shall be treated as CRE exposure.
The sum of the gross positive mark-to-market value of all derivative contracts with respect to a single counterparty shall be considered as current credit exposure.

policies.scope: NBFCs, both at the solo level and at the consolidated (group) level
policies.objective: To identify large exposures, refine the criteria for grouping of connected counterparties and put in place reporting norms for large exposures

policies.policy_description: Framework for NBFCs to manage large exposures and interconnectedness.
policies.objective: To ensure the stability of the financial system by limiting large exposures and preventing contagion.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Relation between Interconnectedness through Control and Interconnectedness through Economic Dependency
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Assess counterparties to identify chain of contagion leading to possible default of all entities.
Calculate exposures according to method prescribed for capital computation, and permit offsetting with credit risk transfer instruments.
Assign zero exposure value to derivatives trading and securities financing transactions outstanding against CCPs, but subject to regulatory reporting requirements.
Report breach immediately and rapidly rectify it; failure to comply may lead to imposition of penalties.
Report large exposures to the Reserve Bank as per reporting template given in Annex XXV.
Determine internal exposure limits on other important sectors and put in place an internal limit for exposure to NBFC sector.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Board-approved policy for adoption of enhanced regulatory framework.
subpolicies.control: Within three months of being advised by the Reserve Bank, NBFC shall put in place a Board-approved policy.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Charting out an implementation plan for adhering to new regulations.
subpolicies.control: The period of three months provided for charting out the plan for implementation shall be subsumed within the 24-months' time-period.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Submission of roadmap as approved by the Board towards implementation of enhanced regulatory requirements.
subpolicies.control: The roadmap shall be submitted to the Reserve Bank and shall be subject to supervisory review.

policies.policy_title: Transition of NBFCs to Upper Layer
policies.policy_description: Regulatory requirements for NBFC-UL
policies.objective: To ensure compliance with enhanced regulatory requirements

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFC shall be eligible to move out of the enhanced regulatory framework only if it does not meet the criteria for classification for five consecutive years.

NBFC-UL may move out of the enhanced regulatory framework before the period of five years if the movement is on account of voluntary strategic move to readjust operations as per a Board-approved policy.

NBFC-UL shall not be eligible to move out of the enhanced regulatory framework if the scaling down of operations is on account of adverse situations specific to the NBFC and its deteriorating financial conditions.

policies.policy_description: Periodic review of methodology for assessing NBFC-UL
policies.objective: To ensure accuracy and effectiveness of assessment methodology

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Methodology for assessing NBFC-UL shall be reviewed periodically.
