policies.objective: To provide guidelines for Financial Companies (IDFs-NBFC) on raising funds, sponsorship of IDF-MFs by NBFCs, and other related matters.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: IDF-NBFC shall raise funds through issue of either rupee or dollar denominated bonds of minimum five-year maturity.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Guidelines governing sponsorship of IDF-MFs by NBFCs
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: All NBFCs shall be eligible to sponsor IDF-MFs with prior approval of the Reserve Bank subject to certain conditions.
subpolicies.control: (i) The NBFC shall have a minimum NOF of ₹300 crore and CRAR of 15 percent; (ii) Its net NPAs shall be less than 3 percent of the net advances; (iii) It shall have been in existence for at least 5 years; (iv) It shall be earning profits for the last three years and its performance shall be satisfactory; (v) The CRAR of the NBFC post investment in the IDF-MF shall not be less than the regulatory minimum prescribed for it; (vi) The NBFC shall continue to maintain the required level of NOF after accounting for investment in the proposed IDF-MF; (vii) There shall be no supervisory concerns with respect to the NBFC.

policies.policy_description: In view of the criticality of the nature of the business model in determining the classification of financial assets and restrictions on subsequent reclassification, NBFCs are advised to put in place Board approved policies that clearly articulate and document their business models and portfolios.
policies.objective: To ensure consistency in the application of accounting standards in specific areas

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall articulate and document their business models and portfolios.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Sales Out of Amortised Cost Business Model Portfolios Policy
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall frame their policy for sales out of amortised cost business model portfolios and disclose the same in their notes to financial statements.

policies.policy_description: NBFCs are advised to put in place sound methodologies for computation of Expected Credit Losses (ECL) that address policies, procedures and controls for assessing and measuring credit risk on all lending exposures.
policies.objective: To ensure accurate estimation of expected credit losses

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The parameters and assumptions considered as well as their sensitivity to the ECL output should be documented. NBFCs are advised not to make changes in the parameters, assumptions and other aspects of their ECL model for the purposes of profit smoothening.
Any adjustments to the model output (i.e. a management overlay) should be approved by the Audit Committee of the Board (ACB) and its rationale and basis should be clearly documented.

policies.policy_description: Ind AS 109 does not explicitly define default, but requires entities to define default in a manner consistent with that used for internal credit risk management. It is recommended that the definition of default adopted for accounting purposes is guided by the definition used for regulatory purposes.
policies.objective: To ensure consistency in the definition of default

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The ACB should approve the classification of accounts that are past due beyond 90 days but not treated as impaired, with the rationale for the same clearly documented.

policies.policy_description: NBFCs shall hold impairment allowances as required by Ind AS and maintain asset classification and compute provisions as per extant prudential norms on Income Recognition, Asset Classification and Provisioning (IRACP).
policies.objective: To ensure adequate provisioning for credit losses

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall appropriate the difference between impairment allowance under Ind AS 109 and provisioning required under IRACP to a separate 'Impairment Reserve'.
subpolicies.control: The balance in the 'Impairment Reserve' shall not be reckoned for regulatory capital, and no withdrawals shall be permitted from this reserve without prior permission from the Department of Supervision of the Reserve Bank.

policies.policy_title: Computation of Regulatory Capital and Regulatory Ratios
policies.policy_description: NBFCs shall determine 'owned funds', 'net owned funds' and 'regulatory capital' in accordance with the specified guidelines.
policies.objective: To ensure compliance with regulatory requirements for capital adequacy

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall not include net unrealised gains arising on fair valuation of financial instruments in owned funds, and all such net losses should be considered.
subpolicies.control: Unrealized gains/losses shall be considered net of the effect of taxation.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs may offset unrealised gains or losses recognised in equity due to own credit risk and cash flow hedge reserve while determining owned funds.
subpolicies.control: Unrealized gain/loss on a derivative transaction undertaken for hedging may be offset against the unrealised loss/gain recognized in the capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Securitised assets not qualifying for de-recognition under Ind AS due to credit enhancement given by the originating NBFC shall be risk weighted at zero percent.
subpolicies.control: The NBFC shall reduce 50 percent of the amount of credit enhancement given from Tier 1 capital and the balance from Tier 2 capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definitions of key concepts used in the norms
Classification and provisioning requirements for project loans
Specific provisions for infrastructure projects

policies.policy_title: Annex III Norms on Restructuring of Advances by NBFCs
policies.policy_description: Framework for restructuring advances by non-banking financial companies (NBFCs)
policies.objective: To facilitate revival of stalled projects and ensure timely repayment of loans

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs may permit extension of debt repayment schedule up to two years in addition to the original debt repayment schedule, subject to certain conditions.
subpolicies.control: Paragraph 3.3.5.3(a)

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Change in Ownership and Extension of Debt Repayment Schedule
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: If a change in ownership takes place before or during the extended debt repayment schedule, the project may be eligible for further extension of debt repayment schedule without classifying the account as non-performing asset.
subpolicies.control: Paragraph 3.3.5.3(b)

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Conditions for Extension of Debt Repayment Schedule
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The extension of debt repayment schedule is subject to certain conditions, including establishment that the project is stalled primarily due to inadequacies of the current promoters, and the new promoters must own at least 51 percent of the paid-up equity capital.
subpolicies.control: Paragraph 3.3.5.3(c)

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Treatment of Financing of Cost Overruns
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Financing of cost overruns beyond the ceiling prescribed in clause (ii) of paragraph 3.3.5.2 above shall be treated as an event of restructuring even if the extension of debt repayment schedule is within the limits prescribed above.
subpolicies.control: Paragraph 3.3.5.3(c)(vii)

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Restructuring of Loans for Non-Infrastructure Projects
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Loans for non-infrastructure projects shall be classified as NPA if they fail to commence commercial operations within one year from the original debt repayment schedule, unless they are restructured and become eligible for classification as 'standard asset'.
subpolicies.control: Paragraph 3.4

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Extension of Debt Repayment Schedule for Non-Infrastructure Projects
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs can prescribe a fresh debt repayment schedule for non-infrastructure projects if the delay in commencement of commercial operations extends beyond one year from the date of completion as determined at the time of financial closure.
subpolicies.control: Paragraph 3.4.3

policies.policy_description: Norms for restructuring of advances by Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs)
policies.objective: To provide guidelines for restructuring of advances to ensure stability and viability of projects

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Extension of DCCO beyond one year
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Extension of date of commencement of commercial operations (DCCO) beyond one year is allowed, subject to certain conditions
subpolicies.control: Financial closure and revised DCCO must be within the prescribed time limits

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Multiple revisions of DCCO are treated as a single event of restructuring, subject to certain conditions
subpolicies.control: Revised DCCO must be fixed within the prescribed time limits and all other terms and conditions of the loan remain unchanged

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs may fund cost overruns, subject to certain conditions, including a maximum limit of 10% of the original project cost
subpolicies.control: Debt Equity Ratio and Debt Service Coverage Ratio must remain unchanged or improve in favour of lenders

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs may permit extension of DCCO up to two years in addition to the prescribed time limits, subject to certain conditions, including a change in ownership and establishment of project viability
subpolicies.control: New promoters must own at least 51% of the paid-up equity capital or up to applicable foreign investment limit, whichever is higher

policies.policy_title: Annex III Norms on Restructuring of Advances by NBFCs
policies.policy_description: Framework for restructuring advances by non-banking financial companies (NBFCs)
policies.objective: To provide a framework for restructuring advances to ensure the viability and sustainability of projects financed by NBFCs

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Extension of Debt Conversion Cumulative Convertible Obligation (DCCO)
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The ceiling prescribed in clause (ii) of paragraph 3.4.5 (b) above shall be treated as an event of restructuring even if the extension of DCCO is within the limits prescribed above.
While considering the extension of DCCO, NBFCs shall make sure that the repayment schedule does not extend beyond 85 percent of the economic life/concession period of the project.
This facility shall be available to a project only once and shall not be available during subsequent change in ownership, if any.
Loans covered under these guidelines shall attract provisioning as per the extant provisioning norms depending upon their asset classification status.

Any change in the repayment schedule of a project loan caused due to an increase in the project outlay on account of increase in scope and size of the project shall not be treated as restructuring if certain conditions are met.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Project Loans for Commercial Real Estate
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Mere extension of DCCO shall not be considered as restructuring, if the revised DCCO falls within the period of one year from the original DCCO and there is no change in other terms and conditions.
NBFCs shall recognise income on accrual basis in respect of projects under implementation, which are classified as 'standard'.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Funded Interest and Conversion into Equity/Debentures
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Income recognition in respect of NPAs shall be done strictly on cash basis, only on realisation and not if the amount of interest overdue has been funded.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Eligibility Criteria for Restructuring of Advances
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs may restructure accounts classified under 'standard', 'substandard' and 'doubtful' categories.
The process of re-classification of an asset shall not stop merely because a restructuring proposal is under consideration.
No account shall be taken up for restructuring unless the financial viability is established and there is a reasonable certainty of repayment from the borrower.

policies.policy_title: Annex III Norms on Restructuring of Advances by NBFCs
policies.policy_description: Framework for restructuring advances by non-banking financial companies (NBFCs)
policies.objective: To provide a framework for restructuring advances by NBFCs

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall determine the viability of borrowers based on acceptable viability parameters and benchmarks.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Borrowers Indulging in Frauds and Malfeasance
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Borrowers who have committed frauds or malfeasance are not eligible for restructuring.
BIFR cases require express approval before restructuring can be considered.

Restructuring of advances shall take place in the following stages: (i) before commencement of commercial production/operation; (b) after commencement of commercial production/operation but before the asset has been classified as 'sub-standard'; (ii) after commencement of commercial production/operation and the asset has been classified as 'sub-standard' or 'doubtful'.

Interest income in respect of restructured accounts classified as 'standard assets' shall be recognized on accrual basis, while interest income in respect of accounts classified as 'non-performing assets' shall be recognized on cash basis.
NBFCs shall hold provision against restructured advances as per extant provisioning norms, and also make provisions for diminution in the fair value of restructured advances.

policies.policy_title: Norms on Restructuring of Advances by NBFCs
policies.policy_description: Framework for restructuring advances by non-banking financial companies (NBFCs)
policies.objective: To ensure accurate calculation of erosion in the fair value of restructured accounts and to provide for the shortfall in provision or reverse the amount of excess provision held in the distinct account.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Calculation of Diminution in Fair Value
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The diminution in the fair value shall be re-computed on each balance sheet date till satisfactory completion of all repayment obligations and full repayment of the outstanding in the account.
subpolicies.control: NBFCs shall put in place a proper mechanism of checks and balances to ensure accurate calculation of erosion in the fair value of restructured accounts.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: In case any security is taken in lieu of the diminution in the fair value of the advance, it shall be valued at ₹1/- till maturity of the security.
subpolicies.control: The effect of charging off the economic sacrifice to the Profit and Loss account is not negated.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Provisioning for Diminution in Fair Value
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The total provisions required against an account (normal provisions plus provisions in lieu of diminution in the fair value of the advance) are capped at 100 percent of the outstanding debt amount.
subpolicies.control: NBFCs shall provide for the shortfall in provision or reverse the amount of excess provision held in the distinct account.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: A part of the outstanding principal amount can be converted into debt or equity instruments as part of restructuring.
subpolicies.control: The debt/equity instruments so created shall be classified in the same asset classification category in which the restructured advance has been classified.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Income, if any, generated by these instruments shall be recognised on accrual basis for standard accounts and on cash basis for non-performing assets.
subpolicies.control: The income recognition norms are applicable to both debt/equity instruments and FITL/debt or equity instruments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: These instruments shall be held under 'current investments' and valued as per usual valuation norms.
subpolicies.control: Depreciation on these instruments shall not be offset against the appreciation in any other securities held under the 'current investment' category.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Conversion of Unpaid Interest into FITL, Debt or Equity Instruments
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The FITL/debt or equity instrument created by conversion of unpaid interest shall be classified in the same asset classification category in which the restructured advance has been classified.
subpolicies.control: The income recognition norms are applicable to both debt/equity instruments and FITL/debt or equity instruments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Following general conditions shall be applicable in all cases of restructuring: ...
subpolicies.control: NBFCs shall decide on the issue regarding convertibility (into equity) option as a part of restructuring exercise...

policies.policy_title: Norms on Restructuring of Advances by NBFCs
policies.policy_description: Framework for restructuring advances by Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs)
policies.objective: To ensure that NBFCs restructure advances in a manner that preserves economic value of units and does not lead to evergreening of problem accounts

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Promoter's contribution need not necessarily be brought in cash and can be brought in the form of conversion of unsecured loan from promoters into equity
NBFCs shall determine a reasonable time period during which the account is likely to become viable, based on cash flow and Techno Economic Viability (TEV) study
NBFCs shall be satisfied that the post-restructuring repayment period is reasonable, and commensurate with estimated cash flows and required DSCR in the account as per their own Board-approved policy
Each NBFC shall clearly document its own due diligence done in assessing TEV and viability of assumptions underlying restructured repayment terms
NBFCs shall disclose information relating to number and amount of advances restructured, and the amount of diminution in fair value of restructured advances in their published annual Balance Sheets
The CDR Mechanism will also be available to corporates engaged in non-industrial activities, if they are otherwise eligible for restructuring as per criteria laid down for this purpose

policies.policy_title: Norms on Restructuring of Advances by NBFCs
policies.policy_description: Framework for restructuring advances by non-banking financial companies (NBFCs)
policies.objective: To ensure timely and transparent mechanism for restructuring corporate debts

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Adherence to conditions during the specified period, including no payment remaining overdue for more than a certain number of days.
No payment shall remain overdue for a period of more than the number of days after which it would be classified as NPA, and there shall not be any overdues at the end of the specified period.
NBFCs shall satisfy themselves about the revenue generation/repaying capacity of the borrower during the entire repayment period, including the extended repayment period.

policies.policy_description: Framework for restructuring corporate debts through a coordinated mechanism involving multiple banks and financial institutions
policies.scope: All borrowers engaged in any type of activity with credit facilities from more than one bank/FI
policies.objective: To ensure timely and transparent mechanism for restructuring corporate debts

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: To preserve viable corporates affected by internal and external factors, minimize losses to creditors and stakeholders, and ensure an orderly and coordinated restructuring programme.
The CDR Mechanism is available to borrowers with total outstanding exposure of ₹10 crore or above from more than one bank/FI.

Representative general body of all financial institutions and banks participating in the CDR system, responsible for laying down policies and guidelines, monitoring progress, and providing an official platform for creditors and borrowers to evolve debt restructuring plans.
Carved out of the CDR Standing Forum, responsible for assisting in convening meetings, taking decisions on policy, and laying down guidelines for debt restructuring.
Decides individual cases of corporate debt restructuring, consisting of ED level representatives from participating financial institutions and banks.

policies.policy_title: Norms on Restructuring of Advances by NBFCs
policies.policy_description: Framework for restructuring advances by non-banking financial companies (NBFCs)
policies.scope: Applicable to all participating institutions and banks in the CDR mechanism
policies.objective: To provide a framework for restructuring advances by NBFCs

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Authorisation of Representatives on the CDR Empowered Group
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The respective Boards of participating institutions/banks shall authorise their representatives to take decisions on behalf of their organisation regarding restructuring of debts of individual corporates.

The CDR Empowered Group shall consider the preliminary report of all cases of requests for restructuring, submitted to it by the CDR Cell. After deciding that restructuring is prima-facie feasible, the detailed restructuring package shall be worked out by the CDR Cell in conjunction with the Lead Institution.
The CDR Empowered Group shall examine the viability and rehabilitation potential of the company and approve the restructuring package within a specified time frame.
The CDR Empowered Group shall decide on acceptable viability benchmark levels based on illustrative parameters such as ROCE, DSCR, IRR-CoF, and extent of sacrifice.
The Board of each bank/FI shall authorise its Chief Executive Officer (CEO) and/or Executive Director (ED) to decide on the restructuring package in respect of cases referred to the CDR system.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Meetings of the CDR Empowered Group
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The CDR Empowered Group shall meet on two or three occasions in respect of each borrowal account, providing an opportunity for participating members to seek proper authorisations from their CEO/ED.

The decisions of the CDR Empowered Group shall be final. If restructuring is approved, the company shall be put on the restructuring mode; otherwise, creditors may take necessary steps for immediate recovery or liquidation.

The CDR Cell shall assist the CDR Standing Forum and the CDR Empowered Group in all their functions, including initial scrutiny of proposals, preparation of detailed rehabilitation plans, and submission to the CDR Empowered Group.

The scheme shall not apply to accounts involving only one financial institution or one bank. The CDR mechanism shall cover multiple banking accounts/syndication/consortium accounts of corporate borrowers with outstanding fund-based and non-fund based exposure of ₹10 crore and above.
The Category 1 CDR system shall be applicable only to accounts classified as 'standard' and 'sub-standard'.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Priority for Potentially Viable Cases of NPAs
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Potentially viable cases of NPAs will get priority under the CDR mechanism.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Review of Reasons for Classification as Wilful Defaulter
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The Core Group shall review the reasons for classification of a borrower as wilful defaulter, especially in old cases where the manner of classification was not transparent.
Exceptional cases involving frauds or diversion of funds with malafide intent shall be admitted for restructuring with the approval of the Core Group only.

policies.policy_title: Norms on Restructuring of Advances by NBFCs
policies.policy_description: Framework for restructuring advances by non-banking financial companies (NBFCs)
policies.scope: Entities outside Strategic Debt Restructuring Scheme
policies.objective: To provide a framework for restructuring advances by NBFCs

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Triggering the Corporate Debt Restructuring (CDR) system and eligibility criteria
Debtor-Creditor Agreement (DCA), Inter-Creditor Agreement (ICA), and 'stand-still' agreement
Provision of additional finance by creditors on a pro-rata basis
Option for lenders to exit the restructuring package and sell their share to other lenders

policies.policy_title: Annex III Norms on Restructuring of Advances by NBFCs
policies.policy_description: Framework for restructuring advances by non-banking financial companies (NBFCs)
policies.scope: Applicable to all creditors and borrowers
policies.objective: To provide a framework for restructuring advances by NBFCs

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Consideration of time settlement as part of the restructuring package
subpolicies.control: If an account with any creditor is subjected to One Time Settlement (OTS) by a borrower before its reference to the CDR mechanism, any fulfilled commitments under such OTS shall not be reversed under the restructured package.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Second category of CDR for cases where accounts have been classified as 'doubtful' in the books of creditors
subpolicies.control: (i) It shall not be binding on the creditors to take up additional financing worked out under the debt restructuring package and the decision to lend or not to lend shall depend on each creditor bank/FI separately. (ii) All other norms under the CDR mechanism such as the standstill clause, asset classification status during the pendency of restructuring under CDR, etc., shall continue to be applicable to this category also.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: All CDR approved packages must incorporate creditors' right to accelerate repayment and borrowers' right to pre-pay, with certain performance criteria
subpolicies.control: Minimum 75 percent of the recompense amount shall be recovered by the lenders and in cases where some facility under restructuring has been extended below base rate, 100 percent of the recompense amount shall be recovered.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Mechanism for restructuring loans availed by Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs)
subpolicies.control: (i) The lender shall formulate, with the approval of their Board of Directors, a debt restructuring scheme for SMEs within the prudential norms laid down by the Reserve Bank. (ii) The main plank of the scheme is that the lender with the maximum outstanding shall work out the restructuring package, along with the lender having the second largest share.

policies.policy_description: To recognize incipient stress in loan accounts and report it to CRILC, and to take corrective action to prevent further deterioration of asset quality.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Recognize incipient stress in loan accounts and report it to CRILC.
subpolicies.control: NBFCs shall recognize incipient stress in loan accounts, immediately on default, by classifying such assets as special mention accounts (SMA) as per the following categories: SMA-0, SMA-1, SMA-2.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Subject notified NBFCs to accelerated provisioning for non-performing accounts.
subpolicies.control: Notified NBFCs shall be subjected to accelerated provisioning for these accounts and/or other supervisory actions as deemed appropriate by the Reserve Bank.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Ensure that credit information is provided to CRILC in a timely manner.

policies.policy_description: To carry out independent and objective credit appraisal in all cases of lending.
policies.objective: To improve credit risk management

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Carry out independent and objective credit appraisal in all cases of lending.
subpolicies.control: Notified NBFCs shall carry out their independent and objective credit appraisal in all cases of lending.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Verify the names of directors of companies to ensure that they are not defaulters.
subpolicies.control: Notified NBFCs shall verify as to whether the names of any of the directors of the companies appear in the list of defaulters by way of reference to DIN/PAN etc.

policies.objective: To facilitate early resolution of stressed assets

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Formation of Joint Lenders' Forum (JLF) for stressed accounts
subpolicies.control: Lender with highest AE convenes JLF, reports account to CRILC as SMA-0

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Development of Corrective Action Plan (CAP) by JLF for stressed accounts
subpolicies.control: JLF explores options for resolution, including rectification, restructuring, and recovery

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Obtaining commitment from borrower to regularize account
subpolicies.control: Identifiable cash flows within required time period, without loss or sacrifice

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Consideration of restructuring option for stressed accounts
subpolicies.control: Commitment from promoters, ICA and DCA signing, 'stand-still' clause

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Initiation of recovery process for stressed accounts
subpolicies.control: Best recovery process to be followed, with view to optimize efforts and results

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Decision-making process within JLF
subpolicies.control: Minimum 75% of creditors by value and 60% by number agree on CAP option

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Procedure for restructuring stressed accounts
subpolicies.control: TEV study, IEC evaluation, and lender approval

policies.policy_description: Framework for restructuring accounts
policies.scope: Applicable to all lenders and borrowers
policies.objective: To ensure the viability of restructured accounts

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The JLF shall prepare a restructuring package for accounts with AE less than ₹500 crore, while accounts with AE of ₹500 crore and above shall be referred to the CDR Cell.

The JLF shall determine the viability of an account based on acceptable viability benchmarks determined by them, including Debt Equity Ratio, Debt Service Coverage Ratio, Liquidity/Current Ratio, and provision required in lieu of diminution in fair value.
If the JLF decides to refer an account to the CDR Cell, the procedure shall be followed as outlined in paragraph 3.4.
The restructuring package shall stipulate timeline for achieving viability milestones, and the JLF must periodically review the account for achievement/non-achievement of milestones.

policies.objective: To ensure adherence to restructuring proposals and maintain credit discipline

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The usual asset classification norm would continue to apply during the restructuring process.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Special Asset Classification Benefit for Quick Implementation of Restructuring Package
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: A special asset classification benefit is available for accounts undertaken for restructuring, subject to certain conditions.
Accelerated provisioning norms are being introduced to ensure adherence to proposals and impose disincentives on borrowers for not maintaining credit discipline.

policies.policy_description: Policy for flexible structuring and refinancing of project loans to infrastructure and core industries projects.
policies.objective: To provide flexibility in loan structuring and refinancing to support the growth of infrastructure and core industries projects.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall fix a Fresh Loan Amortisation Schedule for existing project loans once during the life time of the project, after DCCO, based on reassessment of project cash flows.
subpolicies.control: The loan is a standard loan as on the date of change of Loan Amortisation Schedule; Net present value of the loan remains same before and after the change in Loan Amortisation Schedule; Fresh Loan Amortisation Schedule shall be within 85 percent of initial concession period or economic life.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs may refinance project term loan periodically (5-7 years) after the project has commenced commercial operations.
subpolicies.control: The repayment(s) at the end of each refinancing period shall be structured as a bullet repayment, with intent specified up front that it will be refinanced.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall determine the pricing of loans at each stage of the project term loan or refinancing debt facility, commensurate with the risk at each phase of the loan.
subpolicies.control: The pricing shall be as per the rate approved by the Board.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall secure their interest by way of proper documentation and security creation, etc.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall initially allow cash flows from periodic amortisations of loans as also the bullet repayment of outstanding debt at the end of each refinancing period for their asset-liability management.
subpolicies.control: Conduct behavioural studies of cash flows in such amortisation of loans and plot them accordingly in ALM statements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall recognise from a risk management perspective that there shall be a probability that the loan shall not be refinanced by other lenders, and shall take this into account when estimating liquidity needs as well as stress scenarios.

section_info.title: Annex VI Guidelines on Liquidity Risk Management Framework

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: Ensures that NBFCs maintain sufficient liquidity and a cushion of unencumbered, high-quality liquid assets to withstand a range of stress events.
policies.scope: All non-deposit taking NBFCs with asset size of ₹100 crore and above, Core Investment Companies, and all deposit taking NBFCs
policies.objective: To ensure that NBFCs maintain sufficient liquidity to meet their obligations as they become due without adversely affecting their financial condition.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Ensures the successful implementation of the risk management process by involving top management and the Chief Risk Officer in the identification, measurement, and mitigation of liquidity risks.
subpolicies.control: The Board shall have overall responsibility for managing liquidity risk, and the ALCO shall ensure adherence to the risk tolerance/limits set by the Board.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires NBFCs to clearly articulate a liquidity risk tolerance that is appropriate for their business strategy and role in the financial system.
subpolicies.control: Senior management should develop the strategy to manage liquidity risk in accordance with such risk tolerance.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires NBFCs to quantify liquidity costs and benefits so that they can be incorporated into internal product pricing, performance measurement, and new product approval processes.
subpolicies.control: NBFCs should endeavour to develop a process to quantify liquidity costs and benefits.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Off-balance Sheet Exposures and Contingent Liabilities
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires NBFCs to have a robust framework for comprehensively projecting cash flows arising from assets, liabilities, and off-balance sheet items over an appropriate set of time horizons.
subpolicies.control: The management of liquidity risks relating to certain off-balance sheet exposures should be given particular importance.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires NBFCs to establish a funding strategy that provides effective diversification in the sources and tenor of funding.
subpolicies.control: An NBFC should regularly gauge its capacity to raise funds quickly from each source.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires NBFCs to actively manage their collateral positions, differentiating between encumbered and unencumbered assets.
subpolicies.control: An NBFC should have sufficient collateral to meet expected and unexpected borrowing needs.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires NBFCs to conduct stress tests on a regular basis for a variety of short-term and protracted NBFC-specific and market-wide stress scenarios.
subpolicies.control: An NBFC should conduct stress tests on a regular basis.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires NBFCs to formulate a contingency funding plan for responding to severe disruptions that might affect their ability to fund some or all of their activities in a timely manner and at a reasonable cost.
subpolicies.control: A contingency plan should contain details of available/potential sources of funding.

policies.policy_title: Annex VI Guidelines on Liquidity Risk Management Framework
policies.policy_description: Custom Policy Framework for NBFCs
policies.objective: To ensure sound liquidity risk management framework and position

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Clear escalation/prioritisation procedures detailing when and how each of the actions can and should be activated, and the lead time needed to tap additional funds from each of the contingency sources.

Publicly disclose information on a quarterly basis on the official website of the company and in the annual financial statements as notes to account that enables market participants to make an informed judgment about the soundness of its liquidity risk management framework and liquidity position.
Develop and maintain liquidity management processes and funding programmes that are consistent with the complexity, risk profile, and scope of operations of the companies in the Group.
Have a reliable MIS designed to provide timely and forward-looking information on the liquidity position of the NBFC and the Group to the Board and ALCO, both under normal and stress situations.
Have appropriate internal controls, systems and procedures to ensure adherence to liquidity risk management policies and procedure.
Use a maturity ladder and calculation of cumulative surplus or deficit of funds at selected maturity dates as a standard tool for measuring and managing net funding requirements.
Hold in their investment portfolio, securities which could be broadly classifiable as 'mandatory securities' (under obligation of law) and other 'non-mandatory securities'.
Prepare a Statement of Structural Liquidity by placing all cash inflows and outflows in the maturity ladder according to the expected timing of cash flows.
Adopt a 'stock' approach to liquidity risk measurement and monitor certain critical ratios in this regard by putting in place internally defined limits as approved by their Board.

policies.policy_title: Annex VI Guidelines on Liquidity Risk Management Framework
policies.policy_description: Custom Policy Framework for NBFCs to manage liquidity risk and interest rate risk.
policies.objective: To ensure that NBFCs maintain a stable financial condition by managing liquidity risk and interest rate risk effectively.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Recognize the liquidity risk arising out of foreign assets or liabilities and develop suitable preparedness for managing the risk.
Measure and manage interest rate risk using traditional gap analysis, simulation, and value at risk techniques.
subpolicies.control: Calculate gaps over different time intervals, group rate sensitive liabilities, assets, and off-balance sheet positions into time buckets, and set prudential limits on individual gaps.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Measure mismatches between rate-sensitive liabilities and rate-sensitive assets (including off-balance sheet positions) by calculating gaps over different time intervals.
subpolicies.control: Determine rate sensitivity, classify items into time buckets, and generate a gap report.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Set prudential limits on individual gaps with the approval of the Board/Management Committee.
subpolicies.control: Work out earnings at risk (EaR) or net interest margin (NIM) based on views on interest rate movements and fix a prudent level.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Develop an internal transfer pricing model to assign values to funds provided and funds used, enhancing the management of margin and interest rate risk.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Buckets for Instruments with Embedded Options
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments with embedded options should be placed in respective time buckets as per the next exercise date.
subpolicies.control: Residual maturity or next exercise date

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Deposits and borrowings should be slotted in respective time buckets based on residual maturity or lock-in period.
subpolicies.control: Residual maturity or lock-in period

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Borrowings from others should be placed as per residual period to the repricing date in respective time buckets.
subpolicies.control: Residual period to the repricing date

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Current liabilities and provisions are non-sensitive and do not require bucketing.
subpolicies.control: None required

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Repos/bills rediscounted/forex swaps should be placed as per residual maturity in respective buckets.
subpolicies.control: Residual maturity

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Cash, remittance in transit, and balances with banks in India are non-sensitive. Other assets should be slotted based on residual maturity or timing of cash flows.
subpolicies.control: Residual maturity or timing of cash flows

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Fixed income securities should be slotted as per residual maturity, while floating rate securities should be slotted as per residual time to the repricing date.
subpolicies.control: Residual maturity or residual time to the repricing date

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Bills of exchange and term loans should be slotted based on residual usance or cash flow/maturity, respectively.
subpolicies.control: Residual usance or cash flow/maturity

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Sub-standard and doubtful non-performing loans should be slotted as per the guidelines in Appendix VI-B.
subpolicies.control: Appendix VI-B

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Cash flows on lease assets are sensitive to changes in interest rates and should be slotted based on timing of cash flows.
subpolicies.control: Timing of cash flows

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Fixed assets are non-sensitive and do not require bucketing.
subpolicies.control: None required

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Intangible assets, items not representing cash flows, and other items are non-sensitive and do not require bucketing.
subpolicies.control: None required

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: These instruments should be slotted as per residual maturity in respective buckets.
subpolicies.control: Residual maturity

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Interest rate swaps and other derivatives should be classified suitably based on residual maturity in respective time buckets.
subpolicies.control: Residual maturity

section_info.title: Financial Services by NBFCs

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: Framework for financial services provided by Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs)
policies.objective: To ensure that NBFCs manage risks associated with outsourcing of financial services and maintain customer trust

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definition of outsourcing and its implications for NBFCs
Types of risks associated with outsourcing, including strategic risk, reputation risk, compliance risk, etc.
Criteria for determining material outsourcing arrangements and their impact on NBFCs
Responsibilities of NBFCs in managing outsourced activities, including due diligence and risk management

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Risk Management Practices for Outsourced Financial Services
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements for NBFCs to put in place a comprehensive outsourcing policy and implement robust risk management practices

policies.policy_description: Framework for evaluating risks and materiality of all existing and prospective outsourcing arrangements.
policies.objective: To ensure the stability and reputation of NBFCs through prudent outsourcing practices.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Identifying and assessing potential risks associated with outsourcing, including strategic, reputation, compliance, operational, legal, exit strategy, counterparty, contractual, concentration, and systemic risks.
subpolicies.control: Regular review of outsourcing strategies and arrangements for their continued relevance, safety, and soundness.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Assessing the capability of service providers to comply with obligations in the outsourcing agreement, including financial soundness, business reputation, security, internal control, audit coverage, reporting, and monitoring environment.
subpolicies.control: Due diligence shall involve an evaluation of all available information about the service provider.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defining terms and conditions governing contracts between NBFCs and service providers, including risk mitigation strategies, termination clauses, and controls for customer data confidentiality.
subpolicies.control: The agreement shall be vetted by NBFC's legal counsel on their legal effect and enforceability.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Preserving and protecting the security and confidentiality of customer information, including maintaining confidentiality even after contract expiration or termination.
subpolicies.control: NBFCs shall ensure that service providers preserve documents as required by law and take suitable steps to ensure their interests are protected.

policies.policy_description: Framework for financial services provided by Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs)
policies.objective: To ensure the security and confidentiality of customer information, documents, records, and assets in outsourced activities

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Limit access to customer information by staff of the service provider on a 'need to know' basis

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Isolation and Identification of NBFC's Assets
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Ensure that the service provider is able to isolate and clearly identify the NBFC's customer information, documents, records, and assets
Review and monitor the security practices and control processes of the service provider on a regular basis
Immediately notify the Reserve Bank in the event of any breach of security and leakage of confidential customer-related information

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Responsibilities of Direct Sales Agents/Direct Marketing Agents/Recovery Agents
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Ensure that DSA/DMA/Recovery Agents are properly trained and adhere to a board-approved Code of Conduct

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Business Continuity and Management of Disaster Recovery Plan
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Develop and establish a robust framework for documenting, maintaining, and testing business continuity and recovery procedures

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Monitoring and Control of Outsourced Activities
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Have in place a management structure to monitor and control outsourcing activities, including regular audits and due diligence reviews

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Redress of Grievances related to Outsourced Services
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Constitute a Grievance Redressal Machinery as contained in circular issued by the Reserve Bank on 'Guidelines on Fair Practices Code for NBFCs - Grievance Redressal Mechanism-Nodal Officer'

policies.policy_description: Framework for financial services provided by Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs)
policies.objective: To ensure that NBFCs provide financial services in a responsible and transparent manner

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall have a grievance redressal machinery to deal with customer complaints
subpolicies.control: Time limit of 30 days for customers to prefer their complaints/grievances, and the grievance redressal procedure shall be placed on the NBFC's website

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall report transactions to FIU or other competent authorities
subpolicies.control: Currency Transactions Reports and Suspicious Transactions Reports shall be submitted in respect of customer-related activities carried out by service providers

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs may have back-office and service arrangements/agreements with group entities, but must ensure that these do not compromise their ability to identify and manage risk on a standalone basis
subpolicies.control: Written agreements shall be entered into with details like scope of services, charges for the services, and maintaining confidentiality of customer data

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: NBFCs shall take into account country risk involved in outsourcing activities to foreign countries and establish sound procedures for dealing with country risk problems
subpolicies.control: Government policies, political, social, economic, and legal conditions in countries where the service provider is based shall be closely monitored

section_info.title: Annex XIV Guidelines for Credit Default Swaps - NBFCs as users

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_title: Annex XIV Guidelines for Credit Default Swaps - NBFCs as users
policies.policy_description: Guidelines for credit default swaps (CDS) used by non-banking financial companies (NBFCs)
policies.objective: To ensure the safe and sound operation of CDS contracts between NBFCs and protection sellers

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definitions used in these guidelines for credit default swaps (CDS)
Requirements for the operation of CDS contracts, including direct claims on the protection seller and unconditional payment obligations

control.requirement: CDS contract shall represent a direct claim on the protection seller
control.description: The extent of cover is clearly defined and incontrovertible

control.requirement: No clause in the contract that allows the protection seller to cancel the credit cover or increase the effective cost of cover as a result of deteriorating credit quality
control.description: Protection seller's obligations are not affected by changes in credit quality

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Treatment of Exposures Below Materiality Thresholds
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Materiality thresholds for payments below which no payment is made in the event of loss, and risk weight assignment for capital adequacy purposes
Treatment of credit exposures on underlying assets after a credit event, including ignoring credit protection and maintaining appropriate levels of capital and provisions
Risk weights for credit risk for corporate bonds held by NBFCs, and calculation of counterparty credit risk charge for bought CDS positions

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Treatment of Exposure to the Protection Seller
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Substitution of exposure to the underlying asset with exposure to the protection seller, subject to certain conditions

control.requirement: Operational requirements mentioned in paragraph 2 are satisfied
control.description: Protection seller's obligations are not affected by changes in credit quality

control.requirement: No maturity mismatch between the underlying asset and the deliverable obligation
control.description: Protection seller's obligations are not affected by changes in credit quality

policies.policy_title: Annex XIV Guidelines for Credit Default Swaps - NBFCs as users
policies.policy_description: Framework: Custom Policy Framework
policies.scope: NBFCs as users of credit default swaps
policies.objective: To provide guidelines for the use of credit default swaps by non-banking financial companies (NBFCs)

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Apply risk weights as applicable to the underlying assets for the unprotected portion of the exposure, adjust for mismatches between the underlying asset/obligation and the deliverable asset/obligation.
Adjust credit protection if there are any mismatches between the underlying asset/obligation and the deliverable asset/obligation with regard to asset or maturity.
subpolicies.control: (i) Asset mismatches: ... (ii) Maturity mismatches: ...

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The NBFC shall be eligible to reckon the amount of protection if the maturity of the credit derivative contract were to be equal to the maturity of the underlying asset.
Determine the amount of protection adjusted for maturity mismatch using the formula: Pa = P x (t- .25) ÷ (T- .25)
Recognize protection proportional to the period for which it is available if the residual maturity of the credit derivative contract is three months or more.

NBFCs as users shall adhere to all criteria required for transferring exposures fully to the protection seller in terms of paragraph 6.1 above on an ongoing basis so as to qualify for exposure relief on the underlying asset.

NBFCs shall restrict the total exposure to an obligor including that covered by way of CDS within an internal exposure ceiling considered appropriate by the Board of the NBFC in such a way that it shall not breach the single/group borrower exposure limit prescribed by the Reserve Bank.
NBFCs shall report 'total exposure' on a quarterly basis to the Regional Office of Department of Supervision where they are registered.
NBFCs shall disclose in their notes to accounts of balance sheet the details given in Appendix XIV-A below.

section_info.title: NBFCs

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: NBFCs shall put in place a Board approved policy for resource planning which, inter-alia, shall cover the planning horizon and the periodicity of private placement.
policies.objective: To ensure proper resource planning by NBFCs

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The minimum subscription per investor shall be ₹20,000 (Rupees Twenty thousand).

The issuance of private placement of NCDs shall be in two separate categories, those with a maximum subscription of less than ₹1 crore and those with a minimum subscription of ₹1 crore and above per investor.
There shall be a limit of 200 subscribers for every financial year, for issuance of NCDs with a maximum subscription of less than ₹1 crore, and such subscription shall be fully secured.

There shall be no limit on the number of subscribers in respect of issuances with a minimum subscription of ₹1 crore and above; the option to create security in favour of subscribers shall be with the issuers.
An NBFC shall issue debentures only for deployment of funds on its own balance sheet and not to facilitate resource requests of group entities/parent company/associates.
An NBFC shall not extend loans against the security of its own debentures (issued either by way of private placement or public issue).

section_info.title: being Eligible for Inclusion in Tier 1 capital

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_title: being Eligible for Inclusion in Tier 1 capital
policies.policy_description: Terms and conditions for Perpetual Debt Instruments (PDI) to qualify for inclusion as Tier 1 or Tier 2 capital for capital adequacy purposes.
policies.objective: To ensure that PDI issued by non-deposit taking NBFCs meet the requirements for inclusion as Tier 1 or Tier 2 capital

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: PDI shall be issued in Indian Rupees only, with a minimum investment by single investor in each issue/tranche of ₹5 lakh.
PDI shall be eligible to be treated as Tier 1 capital up to 15% of total Tier 1 capital, with excess amount qualifying as Tier 2 capital.
PDI shall be perpetual.
Interest payable to investors may be either at a fixed rate or at a floating rate referenced to a market-determined rupee interest benchmark rate.
PDI may have a 'call option' subject to strict compliance with certain conditions, and a step-up option for increasing the rate of interest payable on PDIs.
PDI shall be subjected to a lock-in clause in terms of which the issuing NBFC may defer payment of interest under certain circumstances.
Claims of investors in PDI shall be superior to claims of investors in equity shares and subordinated to claims of all other creditors.
PDI instruments shall not be subjected to a progressive discount for capital adequacy purposes since they are perpetual.
PDI shall be fully paid-up, unsecured, and free of any restrictive clauses, and the issue of PDI and its terms and conditions shall comply with certain laws and regulations.

policies.policy_description: Non-deposit taking NBFCs issuing PDI shall submit a report to the Reserve Bank giving details of the debt raised, including the terms of issue specified above.
policies.objective: To ensure that non-deposit taking NBFCs issuing PDI comply with reporting requirements

policies.policy_title: Investment in PDI issued by other NBFCs-ML
policies.policy_description: Non-deposit taking NBFCs investing in PDI issued by other NBFCs and financial institutions shall be subject to certain conditions.
policies.scope: Non-deposit taking NBFCs investing in PDI issued by other NBFCs and financial institutions
policies.objective: To ensure that non-deposit taking NBFCs investing in PDI issued by other NBFCs and financial institutions comply with certain conditions

policies.policy_description: Non-deposit taking NBFCs issuing PDI shall not grant advances against the security of the PDI issued by them.
policies.objective: To ensure that non-deposit taking NBFCs issuing PDI do not grant advances against the security of the PDI issued by them

policies.policy_description: Non-deposit taking NBFCs issuing PDI shall make suitable disclosures in their Annual Report about certain information.
policies.objective: To ensure that non-deposit taking NBFCs issuing PDI comply with disclosure requirements

section_info.title: Annex XXI Guidelines on Liquidity Coverage Ratio (LCR)

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_title: Annex XXI Guidelines on Liquidity Coverage Ratio (LCR)
policies.policy_description: Guidelines for non-deposit taking NBFCs and deposit taking NBFCs to maintain an adequate level of unencumbered High Quality Liquid Assets (HQLA) to meet their liquidity needs.
policies.scope: All non-deposit taking NBFCs with asset size of ₹5,000 crore and above, and all deposit taking NBFCs, regardless of asset size.
policies.objective: To ensure that NBFCs maintain a sufficient level of liquidity to meet their financial obligations during times of stress.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Definition of High Quality Liquid Assets (HQLA)
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Assets that can be readily sold or used as collateral to obtain funds in a range of stress scenarios, and are unencumbered.
The LCR is calculated by dividing the stock of HQLA by total net cash outflows over a 30 calendar-day time horizon under a significantly severe liquidity stress scenario.

Non-deposit taking NBFCs with asset size of ₹10,000 crore and above and all deposit taking NBFCs must maintain a minimum LCR of 50 percent by December 1, 2020, increasing to 100 percent by December 1, 2024.
The stress scenario includes a combined idiosyncratic and market-wide shock that would result in run-off of deposits, loss of unsecured wholesale funding capacity, and other liquidity needs.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Assets to be Included in HQLA
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Cash, government securities, and certain marketable securities issued or guaranteed by foreign sovereigns are included in the computation of HQLAs without any haircut.

policies.policy_title: Annex XXI Guidelines on Liquidity Coverage Ratio (LCR)

policies.scope: This policy applies to all organizational units, personnel, systems, and processes involved in annex xxi guidelines on liquidity coverage ratio (lcr). It includes all operational environments and applies to all stakeholders who may be affected by or responsible for policy implementation.
policies.objective: The primary objective of this policy is to establish clear requirements and guidelines for annex xxi guidelines on liquidity coverage ratio (lcr), ensuring organizational compliance and operational effectiveness. policies.policy_title: Management in NBFCs: Minimum Scope and coverage
policies.objective: Minimum scope and coverage for management in NBFCs

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Constitution, powers, functions, and duties of NRC as per section 178 of the Companies Act, 2013.
Compensation of KMPs and senior management needs to be reasonable, recognising all relevant factors.
All fixed items of compensation, including perquisites and contributions towards superannuation/retiral benefits, may be treated as part of fixed pay.
Variable pay may be in the form of share-linked instruments, or a mix of cash and share-linked instruments.
The proportion of variable pay in total compensation needs to be commensurate with the role and prudent risk taking profile of KMPs/senior management.
Certain portion of variable pay may be deferred to time horizon of the risks.
KMPs and senior management engaged in financial control, risk management, compliance, and internal audit may be compensated in a manner that is independent of business areas they oversee.
Guaranteed bonus may not be paid to KMPs and senior management.
Deferred compensation may be subject to malus/clawback arrangements in the event of subdued or negative financial performance of the company and/or employee misconduct.
